feed.title: blank
feed.subtitle: # A simple, whitespace theme for academics. Based on [*folio](https://github.com/bogoli/-folio) design.

entry.title: Zig-zagging across the universe
entry.summary: What’s better than a single gravitational lens? A double gravitational lens! Learn about how light from a distant quasar zig-zags across the universe because of the first double gravitational lens to be observed!

entry.title: Counting supermassive black hole binaries with customers at a coffee shop

link.title: Counting supermassive black hole binaries with customers at a coffee shop

entry.summary: A short guide on how estimate the number of supermassive black hole binary mergers by measuring how loud it is at your local cafe

entry.title: Can we identify the host galaxies of LISA sources?

link.title: Can we identify the host galaxies of LISA sources?

entry.summary: The upcoming LISA mission promises to detect many gravitational wave events, but can it attribute any of them to a host galaxy? Let’s find out!

entry.title: Celtic Constellations
entry.summary: Today, let’s learn about the Celts and their connections to the night sky!

entry.title: Holy Cow! Is our journey through the universe the reason for the Hubble Tension?

link.title: Holy Cow! Is our journey through the universe the reason for the Hubble Tension?

entry.summary: We move through the universe relative to the Cosmic Microwave Background. Could our movement cause our measurements of the Hubble constant to be biased?

entry.title: Looking for Supermassive Black Holes
entry.summary: The amplitude of the gravitational wave background is too high for the number of supermassive black holes that we’ve observed! Find out how we can resolve this issue in today’s bite

entry.title: Don’t Be So Cold – Self-Interacting Dark Matter as a Solution to the ‘Final Parsec Problem’

link.title: Don’t Be So Cold – Self-Interacting Dark Matter as a Solution to the ‘Final Parsec Problem’

entry.summary: In today’s bite, we look at a paper that investigates if self-interacting dark matter can accelerate the inspiral of supermassive black holes binaries, and if we can detect this with gravitational waves.

entry.title: Meet the AAS Keynote Speakers: Prof. John Peacock

link.title: Meet the AAS Keynote Speakers: Prof. John Peacock

entry.summary: We interview the RAS Gold Medal Lecturer for AAS244, Prof. John Peacock!

entry.title: Book Review: All Through the Night – Why Our Lives Depend on Dark Skies

link.title: Book Review: All Through the Night – Why Our Lives Depend on Dark Skies

entry.summary: A bilingual book review on ‘All Through the Night’ by Dani Robertson. Adolygiad llyfr ‘All Through the Night’ gan Dani Robertson.
